summary: Completed Next.js App Router migration regression testing for required routes, dynamic pages, navigation, contact form flow, and SEO routes. Core frontend behavior is working; major environment/process issue found with supervisor frontend path.

critical.issue: Backend test scope could not be executed because this workspace has no /app/backend service/tests tree despite testing_type=both. Also, platform supervisor frontend config still points to /app/frontend (missing), causing managed frontend process failures.

action_items: Fix supervisor/frontend process config to use current repo path (/app) instead of removed /app/frontend; otherwise preview restarts can leave app down.
If 'both' testing remains required, provide/restore backend service location and API contract for executable backend regression tests.
critical_code_review_comments: Missing data-testid attributes on critical interactive elements (header nav links, footer links, contact form fields/buttons, mobile CTA) reduces test robustness and violates stated testability requirement.
Form success flow in EstimateForm is UI-only (setTimeout + toast) and does not call any API; acceptable only if intentionally static, otherwise integration parity risk for migrated app.
Hardcoded BASE_URL in src/lib/route-seo.ts may drift across environments; consider env-based canonical host if multi-env deployment is expected.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Frontend Next.js routes validated via browser automation: /, /services, /industries, /service-areas, /resources, /case-studies, /contact, dynamic slugs, 404, contact validation/success toast, mobile CTA, robots/sitemap all passed. Main blocker observed was infrastructure/process config: supervisor frontend chdir points to /app/frontend (does not exist), causing ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED until manual yarn dev was started in /app.
rca of the issue: RCA: Preview instability is caused by stale supervisor configuration from pre-migration structure. Repro: stop manual dev process, rely on supervisor-managed frontend, then requests to :3000 fail and frontend.err.log shows 'couldn't chdir to /app/frontend: ENOENT'. Mitigation: update supervisor program directory/command to current project root and Next dev/start command, then validate persistent health after restart.
